name: Cerros da Carrapateira — circular route in Costa Vicentina
name_original: Cerros da Carrapateira — percurso circular na Costa Vicentina
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Bordeira are characterized by a diverse landscape that blends rugged limestone cliffs, expansive golden sand beaches, and vast sand dunes with tranquil inland scrubland, cork oak forests, and verdant valleys. The region, part of the Southwest Alentejo and Costa Vicentina Natural Park, features a mix of coastal paths and riparian forests along stream banks. This varied terrain provides numerous opportunities for outdoor exploration, from dramatic ocean vistas to shaded woodland trails. Best family friendly hiking trails around Bordeira The most popular family friendly hiking route is Pontal da Carrapateira — circular route in Costa Vicentina, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route offers stunning coastal views along the Vicentina Coast. Another top favourite among local hikers is Endiabrada and the Hidden Lakes Trail, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path. It leads through cork forests and past small lakes, offering ample shade. Local hikers also love the Bordeira Beach – Boardwalk to Praia da Bordeira loop from Carrapateira, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes and along a boardwalk, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Bordeira is defined by dramatic coastlines, extensive sand dunes, and serene inland cork oak forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, including easy-going routes perfect for families. The routes in Bordeira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 3000 reviews. More than 13000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bordeira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available around Bordeira?
A: There are over 80 family-friendly hiking trails around Bordeira, with a good mix of easy and moderate options. This guide focuses on routes specifically chosen for families, ensuring manageable distances and enjoyable paths for all ages.

Q: What kind of terrain can we expect on family hikes in Bordeira?
A: Bordeira's family-friendly trails offer a diverse experience, from rugged limestone cliffs and expansive sandy beaches to tranquil inland scrubland and cork oak forests. Many routes feature well-maintained dirt and gravel tracks, with some coastal paths including wooden boardwalks for easier access and stunning views.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, Bordeira offers several excellent circular routes perfect for families. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1420575476/pontal-da-carrapateira-circular-route-in-costa-vicentina" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontal da Carrapateira — circular route in Costa Vicentina&lt;/a&gt;, which provides stunning coastal views. Another great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1421006101/from-bordeira-to-the-sea-circular-route-on-the-vicentina-coast" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Bordeira to the Sea — circular route on the Vicentina Coast&lt;/a&gt;, leading through varied coastal landscapes.

Q: What are the best times of year for family hiking in Bordeira?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most ideal seasons for family hiking in Bordeira. Spring offers the chance to enjoy the region's botanical species in bloom, while both seasons provide temperate conditions that are perfect for outdoor activities. The summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during those months.

Q: Are there any trails that offer shade during warmer months?
A: For trails with more shade, consider routes that venture inland through wooded areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1420566015/endiabrada-and-the-hidden-lakes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Endiabrada and the Hidden Lakes Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a good example, allowing exploration of cork forests and small lakes, providing ample shade during warmer periods.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can we see along the family trails?
A: Bordeira's trails are rich in natural beauty. You can explore the dramatic coastline, vast sand dunes, and the shallow, warm-water lagoon at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/381218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira da Carrapateira Lagoon and Dunes&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer panoramic views from viewpoints like Carrapateira. You might also encounter diverse flora and fauna, including wild herbs that give the area its 'Trail of Aromas' nickname.

Q: Are there any short walks suitable for families with young children?
A: For shorter walks with young children, consider sections of the coastal boardwalks or the shorter versions of trails like the Bordeira Hiking Trail / Trail of Aromas (5 km). The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32173295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordeira Beach – Boardwalk to Praia da Bordeira loop from Carrapateira&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent option, offering easy access and beautiful beach views.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the family-friendly hiking trails?
A: The Southwest Alentejo and Costa Vicentina Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, but it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to help preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about family-friendly trails in Bordeira?
A: The family-friendly trails in Bordeira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning coastal views, the diverse landscapes that keep children engaged, and the well-maintained paths that make hiking accessible for families.

Q: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the trails?
A: The village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/254860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrapateira&lt;/a&gt;, located near many of the trails, offers cafes and restaurants where families can enjoy a meal or refreshments before or after their hike. You'll find options ranging from local eateries to places with views of the coast.

Q: Where can we park when visiting these family-friendly trails?
A: Parking is generally available near the starting points of many popular trails, especially in areas close to beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/212479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia da Bordeira&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/212480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amado Beach&lt;/a&gt;, and in the village of Carrapateira. Look for designated parking areas to ensure easy access to the trailheads.

Q: Are there any stroller-friendly or accessible walks in Bordeira?
A: While many trails involve natural terrain, some sections, particularly the wooden boardwalks along the coast, can be more accessible for strollers. For example, parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32173295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordeira Beach – Boardwalk to Praia da Bordeira loop&lt;/a&gt; offer smoother surfaces. Always check the specific trail conditions if you require full accessibility.
tips: Carrapateira is located in the beautiful nature of the Costa Vicentina Natural Park and is only a stone's throw from the Atlantic, well protected behind an impressive dune landscape. You won't find great shopping or impressive sights here. And that is exactly the highlight! This place is wonderfully original. Here you get to know the real Algarve. Discover local agriculture and fishing, let the wind blow in your face and be enchanted by one of the most beautiful, natural beaches in the Algarve.

A viewpoint demarcated by a concrete construction. From this point there is a privileged view of the southeast of the Costa Vicentina. The place is quite bucolic and peaceful, making it an ideal stopping point. From here onwards the route begins its return.

Almost at the end of the route you have your first view of the coast, after a few kilometers walking through the interior of Costa Vicentina. Praia da Bordeira appears on the horizon, and the beauty of the contrast with the Atlantic is fascinating.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint with Geodetic Marker – Costa Sudoeste
